content.full_text:
RUTAN & TUCKER

ATTORNEYS AT LAW

A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

BANK OF THE WEST, SUITE 1400

611 ANTON BOULEVARD

COSTA MESA, CALIFORNIA 92626-1998

DIRECT ALL MAIL TO: P. O. BOX 1950

COSTA MESA, CALIFORNIA 92626-1950

TELEPHONE (714) 641-5100

(213) 625-7586

TELECOPIER (714) 546-9235

JAMES B. RUTAN (1860-1972)
WILLIAM H. TUCKER, SR. (1896-1980)
WILFORD W. DAHL, SR. (1919-1983)
H. RODGER HOWELL (1925-1989)

December 19, 1991

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-08076

Dear Sir or Madam:

     This office serves as City Attorney for the City of
Lawndale.  We have been instructed by the City Council to
seek advice from the Commission regarding the existence of
any regulations governing the use of a City-owned cable
television channel for political purposes.

     The Lawndale Cable Usage Corporation is a nonprofit
public benefit corporation wholly owned by the City of
Lawndale.  It is organized under the Nonprofit Public Benefit
Corporation Law for charitable purposes, and was formed for
the purposes of promoting the arts and providing public
access for non-commercial educational and cultural television
programs.  (Copies of the Corporation's bylaws and Articles
of Incorporation are enclosed for your review.)  The
Corporation currently allows public access to the cable
channel for a wide variety of subject matter, subject to
restrictions relating to political campaigning and
advertisement, as stated in the Articles of Incorporation.

     The City of Lawndale will hold elections in April of
1992 relating to three Council seats and a City ballot
measure.  There are several candidates for each Council
position, including the current incumbents.  Several persons,
including candidates and citizen-advocates, may wish to
utilize the cable channel during the campaign season to
present information and discuss matters relating to
candidates and campaign issues.

     Given the above, the City requests Commission advice

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
December 19, 1991
Page 2


relating to the existence of any state laws regulating the
use of and access to City-owned cable stations for political
purposes by candidates, advocates for candidates, or
advocates for ballot measures, particularly during the
campaign season.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. If you
have any questions or need any further information, please do
not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely yours,

RUTAN & TUCKER

[signature]

Robert Owen
Assistant City Attorney
City of Lawndale
RO:jl

embedding.qa_text: The letter is from Rutan & Tucker, Attorneys at Law, and it is dated December 19, 1991. The firm lists several attorneys and staff members, including David J. Garibaldi, III, who is noted as "of counsel." The letter is addressed to the Legal Division of the Fair Political Practices Commission in Sacramento, California. The City Attorney's office for the City of Lawndale has been instructed by the City Council to seek advice from the Commission regarding the existence of any regulations governing the use of a City-owned cable television channel for political purposes. The Lawndale Cable Usage Corporation is described as a nonprofit public benefit corporation wholly owned by the City of Lawndale. It is organized under the Nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation Law for charitable purposes, and was formed for the purposes of promoting the arts and providing public access for non-commercial educational and cultural television programs. (Copies of the Corporation’s bylaws and Articles of Incorporation are enclosed for your review.) The Corporation currently allows public access to the cable channel for a wide variety of subject matter, subject to restrictions relating to political campaigning and advertisement, as stated in the Articles of Incorporation. The City of Lawndale will hold elections in April of 1992 relating to three Council seats and a City ballot measure. There are several candidates for each Council position, including the current incumbents. Several persons, including candidates and citizen-advocates, may wish to utilize the cable channel during the campaign season to present information and discuss matters relating to candidates and campaign issues. Given the above, the City requests Commission advice. Legal Division Fair Political Practices Commission December 19, 1991 relating to the existence of any state laws regulating the use of and access to City-owned cable stations for political purposes by candidates, advocates for candidates, or advocates for ballot measures, particularly during the campaign season. Thank you for your attention to this matter. If you have any questions or need any further information, please do not hesitate to contact me. Sincerely yours, RUTAN & TUCKER [signature] Robert Owen Assistant City Attorney City of Lawndale RO:jl
